reasoning: The value on December 1, 2025 (1.457) is the lowest of the past 13 months, representing a sharp drop from November (1.608). Financial variables like the 10-year real interest rate tend to exhibit mean reversion, particularly after hitting a local minimum. Given the historical volatility (monthly changes often exceed 0.1 percentage points) and the current low baseline, an upward bounce is somewhat more likely than further declines in the near term. The FRED tool's combined forecast also leans slightly positive (P=0.55), reflecting a blend of downward momentum and seasonal factors that favor a January increase. For January 2026, I assign a 56% probability of an increase. For March 2026, the forecast horizon is further out, subjecting the series to more potential downward economic trends, so the probability pulls closer to the random-walk baseline of 50%, resulting in 54%.
